column4: Russia annexed Crimea in 2014, backed separatists in Donbas (14,000+ killed pre-2022). Putin launched full invasion citing NATO expansion and "denazification." Largest conventional war in Europe since WWII.icrc+1
column5: Russia (incl. Wagner remnants, North Korean troops in Kursk) vs. Ukraine (backed by NATO/EU with weapons, intel, training).
column6: Russian forces advanced ~10km over 22 months in Donbas at ~15 meters/day. Nearly 1.2 million Russian casualties since Feb 2022. Ukraine recaptured Stepnohirsk (Zaporizhia). Russia launching 62+ long-range missile/drone strikes per week. 45+ civilians killed weekly. Victory Day ceasefire was a tactical pause only.cfr+1
column7: Grinding stalemate trending toward eventual negotiation under external pressure. Russia lacks the combat power to break through decisively; Ukraine lacks the mass to push Russia back to 2014 lines. Combined casualties approaching 2 million by spring 2026. Trump administration is pushing for talks but neither side has incentive to concede territory. Most likely outcome: a frozen conflict along current lines within 12-24 months, with no formal peace treaty--essentially a Korean War-style armistice. Russia emerges economically degraded and strategically diminished but will not admit defeat. Ukraine retains sovereignty over ~82% of its territory but faces a generational reconstruction bill. The longer this grinds, the worse it gets for both sides, but especially for Russia's long-term power projection.cfr+1

column4: Decades of Iranian nuclear brinkmanship, proxy warfare via Hezbollah/Houthis/Iraqi militias, and the post-Oct 7 escalation spiral. Israel and Iran exchanged direct strikes in 2024 (June 2025 "12-Day War"). US joined Israel in a massive joint strike on Feb 28, 2026, killing Supreme Leader Khamenei and destroying missile/naval assets.acleddata+2
column5: USA + Israel vs. Iran (+ Houthi movement, Hezbollah, Iraqi Shia militias). GCC states struck as collateral.
column6: US/Israel destroyed 90%+ of Iran's navy, knocked missile capability down 90% within a week. 250+ Iranian leaders killed. Ceasefire agreed Apr 7-8, 2026 but fragile. IRGC threatened May 20 to expand war "far beyond the region" if strikes resume. Israel has crossed the Litani River in Lebanon. Strait of Hormuz was effectively closed, disrupting 20% of global oil.acleddata+3
column7: The conventional war phase is likely over; the asymmetric and proxy phase is just beginning. Iran's state military capacity is shattered, but the IRGC retains covert capabilities: terror cells abroad, Houthi proxies in the Red Sea, Hezbollah remnants in Lebanon, and sleeper networks across the Gulf. Iran's political succession is in chaos with Khamenei dead and senior leadership decimated. Two scenarios dominate: (1) A weakened successor regime negotiates under duress (40% probability), leading to a constrained but resentful Iran that rebuilds covertly over a decade; (2) IRGC hardliners seize internal control and pursue asymmetric retaliation--shipping disruptions, terror attacks, cyber operations--for years (35% probability). The notion that this war can be "won" cleanly is fantasy. The US/Israel achieved devastating tactical success but created a power vacuum in a country of 88 million with a deeply nationalist population. Gulf economies face years of reconstruction. Global energy markets remain volatile. This is the Middle East's version of the Iraq 2003 problem: winning the war was easy, winning the peace will be nearly impossible.acleddata+2

column3: Broader conflict: 1948; Current Gaza war: 7 Oct 2023
column4: Hamas launched unprecedented attack on southern Israel on Oct 7, 2023, killing ~1,200. Israel responded with massive military campaign in Gaza. Roots trace to 1948 Nakba, 1967 occupation, failed Oslo process, and 17-year Gaza blockade.wikipedia+1
column5: Israel (IDF) vs. Hamas, Palestinian Islamic Jihad. Broader parties: Hezbollah (Lebanon), Houthis (Yemen), Iran (backing).
column6: Netanyahu ordered seizure of 70% of Gaza Strip (May 28, 2026). Top Hamas commander killed by Israeli strike. A Phase 1 ceasefire was agreed earlier but collapsed. ACLED rates Palestine as the most dangerous place on Earth with high violence in ~70% of Gaza/West Bank. Massive displacement, famine conditions.britannica+1
column7: No realistic path to resolution exists under current political conditions. Netanyahu's government is ideologically committed to permanent control of Gaza with no Palestinian state. Hamas is operationally degraded but not destroyed--it will regenerate as an insurgency as it has after every previous war. The humanitarian catastrophe has created a generation radicalized beyond anything seen before. International isolation of Israel is growing but has no enforcement mechanism while the US provides diplomatic cover. Most likely trajectory: indefinite military occupation of Gaza with periodic flare-ups, settler expansion in the West Bank, and the permanent collapse of the two-state framework. The conflict will metastasize regionally, as it already has into Lebanon, Yemen, and Iran.britannica+2

column4: Power struggle between two military factions that jointly overthrew the civilian transitional government. Roots in decades of authoritarian rule (Bashir era), Darfur genocide (2003), and failure of the 2019 democratic transition.acleddata+2
column5: Sudanese Armed Forces (SAF) (Gen. Burhan) vs. Rapid Support Forces (RSF) (Gen. Hemedti). External backers: UAE (RSF), Egypt/Saudi (SAF).
column6: World's worst humanitarian crisis. 150,000+ dead. 14 million displaced (largest displacement crisis globally). 30-33.7 million need humanitarian aid (60%+ of population). Famine spreading. Both sides committing war crimes including sexual violence, ethnic cleansing. No ceasefire in sight. External suppliers continue arming both sides.acleddata+2
column7: This is a conflict the world has chosen to ignore, and it will get worse before it gets better. Neither the SAF nor RSF can achieve decisive military victory. The most probable outcome is de facto partition--SAF controls the Nile Valley and east, RSF controls Darfur and parts of central Sudan--with continued fighting along contact lines for years. A formal ceasefire requires external backers (UAE, Egypt, Saudi) to cut supplies, which none show willingness to do. Sudan is on track to become a failed state of 50 million people, generating refugee flows that will destabilize Chad, South Sudan, Ethiopia, and Egypt for a generation. The international community's response has been catastrophically inadequate. This is the world's most underreported and under-resourced catastrophe.acleddata+2

column3: Military coup: 1 Feb 2021 (broader ethnic conflicts since 1948)
column4: Military (Tatmadaw) seized power from Aung San Suu Kyi's elected government. Triggered nationwide resistance. Myanmar has the world's most fragmented conflict with 1,200+ distinct armed groups.arabcenterdc+3
column5: State Administration Council / Tatmadaw (junta) vs. National Unity Government (NUG) + People's Defense Forces (PDFs) + ethnic armed organizations (Three Brotherhood Alliance: Arakan Army, MNDAA, TNLA, and dozens more). China mediates/arms both sides selectively.
column6: Junta controls only 21% of territory; rebels hold 42%. 96,000+ killed. 3.6 million displaced. Junta held sham elections (Dec 2025-Jan 2026); Min Aung Hlaing installed as president. Junta regaining some momentum near Mandalay after initial rebel gains. Economy gutted. Forced conscription ongoing.arabcenterdc+2
column7: Neither side can win outright, but the junta is slowly consolidating core territory. The resistance made stunning gains in 2023-2024 (capturing regional military commands for the first time in history) but is now facing internal fragmentation, supply problems, and Chinese pressure to accept ceasefires that freeze rebel gains. The junta has air superiority and is using it brutally. Most likely trajectory: a prolonged, multi-decade conflict similar to pre-coup ethnic wars but at higher intensity. China will prevent the junta from collapsing completely (it needs border stability and BRI access) but won't help it reconquer the periphery. Myanmar will remain a patchwork of warlord-controlled territories with no functioning central state for the foreseeable future. The Rohingya remain trapped between the junta and the Arakan Army with no protection.arabcenterdc+2
